## [00:00 - 03:06]

This is going to be a quick overview of the recruiting and hiring process for setters and closers. Now, the first thing I want to talk about is stacking the deck, okay? There's basically going to be two ways for you to find sales reps, and really any team member in your company. There's external, like through a recruiter, like us, okay? And then internal, which is like you finding your own, using your own internal resources. So, like, which one is better? Which one should you do? Well, the answer is both, okay? Now, obviously, a lot of people in the past have just relied on us for recruiting externally. That is it, and they're going to get amazing results. But, you know, I don't really necessarily care that you use our recruiting as much as that you just come in, you invest in this, and you leave on the other end with the result that you really want. So, I always say do both, and that's why I call it stacking the deck. Doing both allows you to stack the deck as much as possible in your favor, okay? And this is especially true if you have a big audience, a big client base, like definitely use the recruiting process that you're going to go over in the next video, and then tap into that in addition to the recruits that we give you, okay? And then just point of address right now, I've had so many conversations with people saying, oh, well, because of X, Y, and Z, my audience would never want to become salespeople. Because I serve doctors, my audience would never want to become salespeople. Because we serve solar companies, our audience would never sell salespeople. I've never found an instance where somebody who didn't have at least some sort of substantial audience, even if it was like a 5,000 person list, like that's it. Maybe they bought the program, they were a client, whatever, who want to become salespeople, okay? So, use us. Like, we have the headhunting, and we're training professional salespeople, so we can always help you and get you great people. But why not stack the deck and see, is there any clients that I have, or past clients, or people in my audience, who already love and are bought into our mission vision values, what we're doing, they bought in the product, or whatever, and they want to like take it to the next level and be a salesperson, okay? Like, why not interview that person, and if everything checks out, give them a shot?
